usage_in_stream.mechanism: stream_options.include_usage=true yields an OpenAI-conformant extra final chunk with choices:[] and a usage object (observed). The chunk also carries non-standard extras (timings, usage.prompt_tokens_details) that the adapter strips before relay.

cancellation.propagation_note: Closing the upstream HTTP connection makes llama-server log 'srv stop: cancel task' and free the slot; the abort is noticed at the next decode-batch boundary (observed release: single-digit ms mid-stream in decode; up to ~1.3 s when canceled inside a 2048-token prefill batch of a ~3600-token prompt). Teardown must be a real TCP FIN/RST — a half-closed socket keeps the slot generating to natural completion (probed 2026-07-10).

provenance.notes: Probed against a locally built llama-server (commit 8f114a9, 'version: 1 (8f114a9)', GNU 13.3.0, Linux x86_64) serving an 8.9 MB random-weight llama-architecture GGUF (F32, llama SPM vocab, fallback ChatML template) in the IG-T005 reference configuration: -np 2 -c 8192 --metrics --slots (2 slots x 4096 ctx -> context_limit_tokens=4096 per request, max_concurrent_requests=2; requests beyond slot count are deferred, visible as llamacpp:requests_deferred). Facts observed and relied on by the adapter: unknown request fields silently accepted (never rejected) - the gateway's strict validation is the contract boundary; 'model' field ignored, responses echo the loaded alias (adapter normalizes); non-standard response extras timings / system_fingerprint / usage.prompt_tokens_details (adapter strips); streaming 'created' stamped per chunk (std::time(0) at emit, server-task.cpp) so it drifts across a wall-clock second mid-stream (adapter pins the first chunk's value); /metrics exposes exactly the 11 unlabeled 'llamacpp:'-prefixed series in name_mapping (colon in metric names - some scrapers dislike this) with NO kv-cache-usage or per-request latency series - per-request timings come from the stripped timings field or /slots; prompt caching on by default (cached_tokens > 0 on repeats; disable server-side with --no-slots-cache or per request with cache_prompt:false, llama.cpp-native param). max_batched_tokens: null - n_batch is 2048 by default but was not verified against this running build. quantization.methods_supported is source-reported (GGUF loader), not probed; active=null because the probe model is unquantized F32. Tiny-model artifact, not an engine capability: random weights occasionally emit invalid UTF-8 that the server's chat-output parser rejects with HTTP 500 ('peg-native format') at end of generation, measured ~3% of generations. Engine metric names and endpoint shapes are volatile: as of 2026-07 - re-verify at use time.
